title: Bourreria fasciculata
taxonomy: Bourreria fasciculata is classified in the family Boraginaceae. This family includes a large number of flowering plant species commonly known as borage or forget-me-nots. The Boraginaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes, with Bourreria fasciculata belonging to one of these classifications. Bourreria fasciculata is a genus within the family and is native to tropical regions. Further classification of Bourreria fasciculata at the species level may involve the consideration of its botanical name and classification within a specific genus or tribe within the Boraginaceae family.

intro: Bourreria fasciculata, also known as Guarea or White Guarea, is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Boraginaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in various folk medicine systems for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties, which may aid in treating wounds, infections, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Bourreria fasciculata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Bourreria fasciculata is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, cough, and skin infections.
